title: Frequently Asked Questions
subtitle: Answers to the most common questions about regression therapy

q1.question: Is regression therapy safe?
q1.answer: Yes, regression therapy is a safe method when guided by a qualified professional. During the session, you always remain in control and can return to your normal state of consciousness at any time.

q2.question: What if I don’t recall anything during the session?
q2.answer: This can happen, but it doesn’t mean the session was ineffective. Sometimes the information emerges later as insights, dreams, or sudden realizations. Healing can occur on a subconscious level even without clear memories.

q3.question: How many sessions are needed to resolve my issue?
q3.answer: The number of sessions depends on the nature of the issue and your readiness for change. Some problems can be resolved in 1–2 sessions, others may require longer work. After the first session, we can better evaluate your needs.

q4.question: Can regression sessions be done online?
q4.answer: Yes, regression therapy sessions are effectively conducted online. It’s important to ensure good sound quality, a stable internet connection, and a comfortable, undisturbed space.

q5.question: Can everyone enter a regression state?
q5.answer: Most people can enter a regression state, though the depth may vary. Some enter deep trance easily, others stay in a lighter state — which is still enough for effective therapy.

q6.question: Can I ‘make up’ memories during regression?
q6.answer: This is a common concern. During regression, your subconscious presents information in the most accessible form — memories, symbols, metaphors, or sensations. It’s important not to analyze during the session, just observe. The therapist will help you distinguish imagination from real memory.

q7.question: Are there any contraindications for regression therapy?
q7.answer: Regression therapy is not recommended for people with severe mental disorders such as schizophrenia, acute bipolar disorder, or epilepsy. Sessions should also not be conducted under the influence of alcohol or drugs.

contactNote: Have other questions? Feel free to contact me for more information.
